candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue line with populated values; preferable to header/subtotal rows.
Also a revenue line, but appears to be a subtotal/total; useful as fallback if label changes by year.
Section/header, not the numeric revenue line.

variables.notes: Using revenue lines as a priority list, with the direct reported revenue line first and the subtotal revenue line second.

candidates.why: Best direct COGS/cost of sales line; populated and more specific than total duplicates.
Duplicate total matching cost of operating revenue; less preferred than the direct line.
Could be useful conceptually, but not the primary reported COGS line across years.
Appears to cover earlier years but is not available in 2025 and is less clean than the primary COGS line.

variables.notes: Selected the most direct cost-of-sales line and avoided duplicate total rows.

candidates.row_label: Selling, General & Administrative Expenses excluding Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Best semantic match for SG&A overhead excluding R&D; ideal for PROF because formula already subtracts XRD.
SG&A-related but less complete/explicit than the excluding-R&D line.
Too broad and includes COGS; not suitable when explicit SG&A exists.
R&D line exists separately, but should not be included when using explicit SG&A excluding R&D for PROF.

variables.notes: CR:c|low|missing. Chose the explicit SG&A row that already excludes R&D, which is the cleanest input for PROF and avoids double subtraction.

candidates.why: Dedicated R&D expense line above EBIT.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Expense - Expensed & Capitalized - Total - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental R&D disclosure; less preferred than the primary income statement line.
Supplemental duplicate of the main R&D amount.

candidates.row_label: Selling, General & Administrative Expenses excluding Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Not an R&D expense row; it explicitly excludes R&D.

variables.notes: SG&A selection explicitly excludes R&D, so XRD is left empty per the rule to avoid subtracting R&D twice in the PROF formula.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
candidates.why: Best recurring interest expense line; preferred over net finance cost totals and over duplicated generic interest row.
Also a direct interest line and numerically identical here, but less specific than the capitalized-interest-adjusted line.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supple
candidates.why: Recurring financing cost, but likely already embedded in total interest expense; including it risks double counting.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest

variables.notes: Selected the direct recurring interest expense line and avoided total net finance cost rows and potential lease-interest double counting.

candidates.row_label: Shareholders' Equity - Attributable to Parent Shareholders - Total
candidates.why: Best book equity line because it explicitly excludes non-controlling interests.

candidates.row_label: Common Equity Attributable to Parent Shareholders
candidates.why: Equivalent parent-only common equity line; good fallback if label changes.
Appears equivalent here, but parent-attributable lines are more explicit and preferred.

candidates.row_label: Total Shareholders' Equity - including Minority Interest & Hybrid Debt
candidates.why: Too broad and could include minority interest/hybrid debt; only use if no better owner-equity line exists.

final_choice.row_label: Shareholders' Equity - Attributable to Parent Shareholders - Total
Common Equity Attributable to Parent Shareholders

variables.notes: Using parent-attributable equity lines as priority choices so BE excludes any minority interest.

candidates.row_label: Total Shareholders' Equity - including Minority Interest & Hybrid Debt
candidates.why: Only balance-sheet candidate mentioning minority interest, but values equal parent equity and no separate NCI line is shown.
Income statement line, not a balance sheet minority interest stock value.
Income statement line, not MIB.

variables.notes: No separate minority interest/non-controlling interests balance sheet line is present. Total equity including minority interest equals parent equity, indicating MIB is effectively zero/not reported separately.
